strengths: Completed all required phases including proper traction setup and circle following
Achieved solid completion time of 81 seconds with no penalty deductions
Maintained consistent instrument control throughout the cutting sequence
weaknesses: Cutting speed could be more efficient to approach intermediate benchmarks
Traction positioning may need refinement for optimal scissor entry angles
Circle following precision could be improved to minimize any edge deviations

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining steady traction, focusing on smooth transitions between segments.
drills.targets_weakness: Circle following precision and cutting speed efficiency.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: Practice 15 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to achieve optimal scissor entry angles before beginning cuts.
drills.targets_weakness: Traction positioning for optimal scissor entry angles.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion into faster, more precise execution. Your 81-second time shows good control - now work on approaching the 60-second benchmark while maintaining accuracy.
